hero.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker
hero.description: A dark fantasy open-world action RPG set in 14th-century Europe, following Coen as he shifts between human and vampire while choices reshape Vale Sangora.

gameFeature.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Explore Vale Sangora, manage Coen's human and vampire sides, and follow a choice-driven dark fantasy RPG built around consequence.

releaseDate.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Release Date
releaseDate.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker launches on September 3, 2026 for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. Steam storefront date display may show September 2, 2026 in some regions because platform storefronts can localize release timing.
releaseDate.intro: Use this section to track the official launch date, platform availability, storefront status, and regional date display for The Blood of Dawnwalker. The game is available for pre-order and wishlist tracking across supported platforms.

items.label: Official Global Launch Date
items.description: Bandai Namco and Rebel Wolves list September 3, 2026 as the official launch date.

items.label: Steam Store Date Display
items.description: Steam search and storefront metadata display Sep 2, 2026 in some regions, while the official global date remains September 3, 2026.

items.label: Launch Platforms
items.description: The game is planned for current-generation console platforms and PC at launch.

items.label: Storefront Status
items.description: Digital pre-orders are live on Xbox and Steam, with official platform and publisher pages available for wishlisting and purchase.

preOrderAndEditions.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Pre Order and Editions
preOrderAndEditions.subtitle: Compare Standard Edition, Eclipse Edition, Collector's Edition, and retail Day One Edition before buying. Pre-order bonuses include the Sangoran Wayfarer's Armor Set, which is also obtainable later in-game.
preOrderAndEditions.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker is available in multiple editions aimed at different buyers: Standard Edition for the base game, Eclipse Edition for digital extras, Collector's Edition for physical collectibles, and Day One Edition for retail disc buyers.

items.price: $69.99+ on Xbox US; EUR 69.99 on Bandai Namco EU Store
items.included: The Blood of Dawnwalker base game; pre-order bonus Sangoran Wayfarer's Armor Set
items.best_for: Players who only want the full game and the pre-order armor bonus

items.price: $79.99+ on Xbox US; EUR 79.99 on Bandai Namco EU Store
items.included: Base game; Sangoran Wayfarer's Armor Set; Digital World Compendium, Digital Comic Book, and Digital Soundtrack
items.best_for: Players who want lore extras, soundtrack content, and digital bonus materials

items.format: Physical collector package with digital upgrade content
items.price: EUR 199.99 on Bandai Namco EU Store
items.included: Base game; Steelbook; World Map; PureArts Coen Figurine; Physical World Compendium; Gold-Laminated Sticker Set; Eclipse Edition Content; Sangoran Wayfarer's Armor Set
items.best_for: Collectors who want physical display items and the full digital bonus package

items.price: Retail edition pricing depends on store and region
items.included: Retail Day One Edition announced for worldwide stores
items.best_for: Players who prefer a physical retail copy at launch

platforms.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Platforms
platforms.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker is confirmed for PC, Steam, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. Xbox buyers also get Xbox Play Anywhere support across Xbox console and PC.
platforms.intro: This platform section helps players quickly choose where to buy or wishlist The Blood of Dawnwalker. It separates Steam PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Xbox Play Anywhere features so users can understand what each storefront offers.

items.features: Pre-purchase and wishlist support; PC system requirements listed; Steam release display may show Sep 2, 2026 by region
Offline play enabled, one-player support, DualSense vibration support, and accessibility features listed on the PlayStation page
Optimized for Xbox Series X|S, 4K Ultra HD, ray tracing, Xbox achievements, Xbox cloud saves, and single-player support
Buy once and play on Xbox console and PC for no additional cost within the Xbox ecosystem

systemRequirements.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker System Requirements
systemRequirements.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker PC requirements cover Minimum, Recommended, and Ultra targets from native 1080p 30 FPS up to native 2160p 60 FPS. All listed tiers require Windows 10, DirectX 12, 16 GB RAM, and 60 GB SSD storage.
systemRequirements.intro: Use this section to check whether a PC can run The Blood of Dawnwalker at the target resolution and frame rate. The published requirements were measured without upscalers or frame generation, while DLSS and FSR support can help improve performance.

items.cpu: Intel Core i7-8700K or AMD Ryzen 7 3700X
items.gpu: NVIDIA RTX 3050, GTX 1070, AMD RX Vega-56, or Intel Arc A580

items.cpu: Intel Core i5-13600 or AMD Ryzen 9 7900X
Intel Core i5-13600 or AMD Ryzen 9 7900X
items.gpu: NVIDIA RTX 4070 Ti or AMD RX 7800 XT

items.gpu: NVIDIA RTX 4080 or AMD RX 7900 XTX

story.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Story
story.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker follows Coen, a young Dawnwalker caught between humanity and vampiric power, as he fights to save his family in Vale Sangora.
story.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker is set in a brutal 14th-century Europe weakened by war and the Black Death. As vampires seize power, Coen is forced into a rescue story shaped by alliances, revenge, time pressure, and the cost of every decision.

items.title: 14th-Century Europe in Collapse
items.content: Bloody conflicts and the Black Death leave Europe vulnerable. Vampires use this moment of weakness to step out of the shadows, claim power, and turn old legends into living threats.

items.title: Coen, the Dawnwalker
items.content: Coen is a young man transformed into a Dawnwalker: human by day and vampire by night. His story centers on whether he fights for his humanity or embraces cursed power to save the people he loves.

items.title: Brencis and the Rule of Blood
items.content: Brencis is the vampire lord whose power dominates Coen's homeland. Coen's family is pulled into Brencis' rule, and the village faces a system of vampiric protection paid for in human blood.

items.title: Vale Sangora and Svartrau
items.content: Vale Sangora is a mysterious Carpathian region filled with settlements, forests, caves, swamps, ruins, and ancient secrets. Its capital, Svartrau, is the heart of Brencis' power and a key hub for Coen's investigation.

items.title: Thirty Days and Nights
items.content: Coen has 30 days and 30 nights to save his kin. Major actions, quest completions, and certain choices move time forward, creating consequences without turning exploration into a real-time countdown.

items.title: Myths, Monsters, and Hidden Tales
items.content: Vale Sangora contains myths such as the Howling Keep, Shrike's Crag, underground ruins beneath Svartrau, and monsters beyond vampires. These stories turn the region into a playable mystery, not just a backdrop.

gameplay.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Gameplay
gameplay.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker lets you explore Vale Sangora, choose your route through the narrative sandbox, and use Coen's human and vampire sides to solve quests in different ways.
gameplay.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker gameplay combines open-world exploration, choice-driven quests, cinematic combat, time-cost decisions, and two distinct day-and-night loops. Progress is about weaving Coen's version of the story.

items.title: Explore Vale Sangora
items.description: Roam a hand-crafted open world with forests, plains, swamps, caves, mountains, medieval settlements, ancient crypts, ruins, shrines, camps, monster nests, hidden treasures, and haunted places.
items.player_value: The world is designed for discovery, with many activities revealed through clues, locals, travel, and careful searching.

items.title: Choose Your Story Route
items.description: Pursue the rescue of Coen's family, prepare for greater challenges, build alliances, uncover ancient secrets, or push directly toward Brencis when the story allows it.
items.player_value: The narrative sandbox lets players decide what to prioritize and what to leave unresolved.

items.title: Manage Time Costs
items.description: Exploration and combat do not continuously move the clock. Time advances when certain activities are completed, including quests, specific choices, and shifting from day to night.
items.player_value: The 30-day rescue clock creates urgency while still allowing players to explore at their own pace.

items.title: Switch Between Human and Vampire Play
items.description: Human Coen uses swordplay and ritual magic during the day, while vampire Coen gains supernatural movement, heightened senses, claws, feeding options, and night-focused abilities.
items.player_value: Different times of day create different routes, quest opportunities, and ability choices.

items.title: Fight with Swords, Hexes, and Claws
items.description: Combat supports grounded swordfighting, directional attacks, blocks, parries, active abilities, hexes such as Burning Blood, vampiric claws, and close-range decision making.
items.player_value: Players can lean toward cinematic action, precise directional combat, magic, vampire aggression, or mixed builds.

items.title: Build Coen Through Skill Trees
items.description: Coen's progression includes human abilities, vampire abilities, and shared skills. Some powers are passive, some are active, and some vampire-linked powers are connected to specific blood.
items.player_value: The build system rewards replaying the game with different priorities, forms, routes, and consequences.

dayNightMechanics.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Day and Night Mechanics
dayNightMechanics.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker day-and-night system changes Coen's abilities, routes, risks, and how the world reacts to him.
dayNightMechanics.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker treats day and night as more than visual atmosphere. Each time state gives Coen different tools, different weaknesses, different quest access, and different pressure on the 30-day rescue clock.

items.title: Daytime Human Loop
items.content: During the day, Coen leans on swordplay and ritual magic. Magic requires runes carved into the body, giving daytime Coen access to spells that can damage enemies, manipulate encounters, reveal information, and support investigation.

items.title: Nighttime Vampire Loop
items.content: At night, Coen's vampire side gains a different set of abilities. Shadowstep, Clawride, Planeshift, heightened senses, claws, feeding, and supernatural traversal let him approach rooftops, interiors, and combat scenarios in new ways.

items.title: Time Does Not Tick Like a Normal Clock
items.content: The game does not use a constantly running real-time clock. Exploration and combat can be done at the player's pace, while certain completions and decisions push the world forward with visible time costs.

items.title: Different Times Unlock Different Paths
items.content: Some NPCs, quests, routes, and solutions are tied to the time of day. One gameplay example sends Coen back to a cathedral at night because his vampire traversal is needed to reach high scaffolds.

items.title: Blood Hunger at Night
items.content: Blood Hunger activates at night. If Coen does not drink enough blood, he can lose control and attack NPCs, while feeding choices can include humans, animals, and non-human creatures.

items.title: A World That Changes After Sunset
items.content: Vale Sangora reacts to Coen's actions and becomes more dangerous after dark. Places that seem quiet in daylight can become threat-filled at night, and dynamic weather such as fog can affect visibility.

combatMagic.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Combat and Magic
combatMagic.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker combat is built around Coen's split nature: grounded swordfighting and rune-based magic by day, supernatural claws and vampiric powers by night.
combatMagic.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker combat system blends close-range action, directional attacks, blocks, parries, active abilities, ritual spellcasting, vampire traversal, feeding pressure, and flexible skill trees.

items.title: Grounded Sword Fighting
items.description: Coen fights with swords taught by his father, using directional attacks, blocks, and parries to exploit enemy openings. Combat is designed to feel cinematic, close-range, responsive, and not locked into long animations.

items.title: Activation Charges and Active Abilities
items.description: Successful blocking, attacking, and parrying build Activation Charges. These charges can be spent on Active Abilities, including magical and mundane options such as Burning Blood and Artery Attack.

items.title: Rune-Based Hex Magic
items.description: Human Coen casts magic by carving runes into his flesh, opening a gate for power to flow. Known spell examples include Burning Blood, Feral Mind, buffs, debuffs, and Compel Soul for gathering secrets from the dead.

items.title: Vampire Claws and Night Powers
items.description: At night, Coen uses vampiric claws in combat and gains supernatural powers such as Shadowstep, Clawride, Planeshift, heightened senses, and instinctive energy channeling instead of book-learned spells.

items.title: Blood Hunger and Feeding
items.description: Blood Hunger is a night mechanic tied to Coen's vampire side. Feeding can target humans, animals, or non-human creatures, and low blood can push Coen toward losing control and draining NPCs.

items.title: Three-Branch Skill Growth
items.description: Coen's skill tree is built around witchcraft for daytime, vampiric powers for night, and sword fighting that overlaps both forms. Upgrades include passive and active options, with some abilities tied to specific vampiric blood.

choicesConsequences.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Choices and Consequences
choicesConsequences.subtitle: Every major decision, delayed action, and failed rescue can reshape Coen's journey through Vale Sangora. This module explains how quests, allies, blood hunger, and faction choices affect the world around you.
choicesConsequences.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker is built around a narrative sandbox where action and inaction both matter. Coen can rush toward his family, prepare through other stories, help villagers, ignore requests, feed carefully, or give in to darker powers, and the game continues even when those choices carry painful consequences.
choicesConsequences.highlightTitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker Consequence Highlights

highlights.label: Action and inaction
highlights.detail: The Blood of Dawnwalker treats delayed rescues, skipped requests, and direct intervention as story choices.

highlights.label: Limited time
highlights.detail: The 30-day deadline means helping one person can cost time that another quest, ally, or family objective may need.

highlights.label: Blood Hunger
highlights.detail: Coen's night form can turn feeding pressure into a consequence chain that affects NPCs and future scenes.

highlights.label: Faction routes
highlights.detail: The Blood of Dawnwalker leaves room for alliances, vampire encounters, and world reactions beyond a single morality track.

items.question: How do choices work in The Blood of Dawnwalker?
items.answer: Choices are tied to quests, dialogue, timing, allies, and Coen's human or vampire nature. Players can pursue multiple objectives, ignore some requests, prepare before confronting Brencis, or take a more direct route, with the world reacting to the path they choose.

items.question: Does inaction have consequences in The Blood of Dawnwalker?
items.answer: Yes. The game emphasizes that every action and inaction shapes the world. Ignoring or delaying certain objectives leads to visible consequences for villagers and family members, while the story keeps moving forward instead of resetting the outcome.

items.question: Can NPCs die in The Blood of Dawnwalker because of player decisions?
items.answer: Minor and major NPCs can suffer or die as a result of player choices, missed opportunities, or the cost of time. The game continues after these events, turning those outcomes into part of the player's version of Coen's story.

items.question: Is The Blood of Dawnwalker a simple good or evil morality system?
items.answer: The game does not frame morality as a clean black-and-white meter. Many storylines are designed around ambiguity, difficult trade-offs, and shades of gray, so players judge what is worth sacrificing and who deserves help.

items.question: How does Blood Hunger affect The Blood of Dawnwalker decisions?
items.answer: At night, Coen's Blood Hunger can push him toward dangerous behavior. If he does not drink enough blood, he may lose control and attack NPCs, and feeding choices can affect whether victims survive, pass out, or become part of a darker consequence chain.

items.question: Can you work with vampires or other factions in The Blood of Dawnwalker?
items.answer: The Vrakhiri are central enemies and rulers of Vale Sangora, but the game suggests not every vampire encounter has to end the same way. Some decisions may allow Coen to understand parts of the vampire side or form unlikely alliances.

items.question: Do choices affect The Blood of Dawnwalker endings?
items.answer: Choices, paths, consequences, character outcomes, and the use of limited time are designed to significantly affect the plot and ending. Since players cannot complete every story arc before the deadline, different playthroughs can produce different routes and resolutions.

timeLimit.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker 30 Days Time Limit
timeLimit.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker gives Coen 30 in-game days and nights to save his family, but the timer is not a real-time countdown. Time moves through meaningful progress, not ordinary exploration.
timeLimit.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker uses time as a story resource. You can explore Vale Sangora at your own pace, but completing quests, choosing certain dialogue outcomes, changing from day to night, and some major progression actions can move the world closer to the deadline.

summary.label: The Blood of Dawnwalker exploration rule
summary.detail: Exploration, combat, and searching the world do not drain the timer just because real-world minutes pass.

summary.label: The Blood of Dawnwalker visible time costs
summary.detail: Some quests, dialogue outcomes, and day-night changes show a clear time cost before commitment.

summary.label: The Blood of Dawnwalker consequence rule
summary.detail: Running out of time does not simply reset the story; the world continues with the weight of Coen's priorities.

items.title: Start with Coen's deadline
items.description: Coen has 30 days and 30 nights to save his family from the vampire monarchy. The deadline creates urgency around the main goal without turning the game into a constant real-time race.

items.title: Explore without draining the clock
items.description: Exploration, combat, searching the world, and moving through locations do not continuously push time forward. Players can investigate caves, settlements, swamps, roads, ruins, and clues without losing time simply because real-world minutes pass.

items.title: Watch for visible time costs
items.description: Certain completed activities have a specific time cost shown before commitment, often represented with an hourglass-style indicator. These costs help players decide whether a quest, choice, or action is worth spending precious time on.

items.title: Complete quests carefully
items.description: Finishing quests is one of the main ways time advances. Some side quests, story objectives, and important choices can move the calendar forward, so players must choose whom to help and what to leave unfinished.

items.title: Use day and night strategically
items.description: Changing from day to night can advance time, but it also changes Coen's available abilities, traversal options, quests, and risks. Human Coen and vampire Coen open different routes through the same world.

items.title: Accept that not everything fits into one run
items.description: The time system is designed so players cannot complete every story and arc before the deadline. This makes preparation, route planning, alliances, and skipped objectives part of the role-playing experience.

items.title: Consequences replace a hard game over
items.description: When the deadline runs out, the game does not simply end. Coen's family may suffer consequences, but the world and story continue, carrying the weight of what the player chose to prioritize.

openWorldMap.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Open World and Map
openWorldMap.subtitle: Vale Sangora is a handcrafted dark fantasy region in the Carpathian Mountains, filled with settlements, forests, swamps, mountain paths, caves, ruins, and hidden threats. This The Blood of Dawnwalker module highlights known locations and exploration rules.
openWorldMap.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker takes place in Vale Sangora, a fictional valley inspired by Central and Eastern Europe. Its world is designed around discovery: the map can be explored through clues, local information, points of interest, and day-night changes that alter danger, access, and atmosphere.

items.title: Vale Sangora
items.description: The main region of the game, located within the Carpathian Mountains. It is a secluded valley shaped by vampire rule, old myths, dangerous routes, ancient places, and a living world that reacts to Coen's choices.

items.title: Svartrau
items.description: The capital of Vale Sangora and the heart of Brencis' power. It is described as the only true city, with specialized merchants, crowded districts, underground spaces, and major story importance.

items.title: Silberkreis
items.description: A wealthy district near Castle Greifberg, shown during gameplay as Coen travels through Svartrau. It connects the city's social hierarchy with Brencis' control over the region.

items.title: Castle Greifberg
items.description: The castle associated with Brencis, the vampire ruler of Vale Sangora. Coen can attempt to confront this seat of power early, but doing so without preparation creates a sharper challenge.

items.title: Primeval Forests and Tantari Woods
items.description: Vale Sangora includes dense forests filled with herbs, hidden knowledge, monsters, and local legends. Tantari Woods is tied to the tale of the Howling Keep, a mysterious mansion said to appear or vanish depending on intent.

items.title: Swamps, Caves, Mines, and Ruins
items.description: The world includes treacherous swamps, dark underground caves, haunted mines, ancient crypts, timeless ruins, and forgotten remnants of earlier civilizations. These areas support exploration, danger, and secrets beneath the surface.

items.title: Shrike's Crag
items.description: A cursed quarry linked to the violent history of Skender Dragosti and a crushed peasant rebellion. It represents the kind of local legend and tragic landmark players can uncover while exploring Vale Sangora.

items.title: Dynamic Weather
items.description: Weather is dynamic and can affect visibility, such as fog making it harder to see. The world also changes in feel between day and night, making familiar paths more dangerous after sunset.

items.title: Map Discovery Rules
items.description: The full map is visible from the start, but important activities must be discovered before they are fully marked. Entering a new area can reveal activity indicators, while exact details require clues, local information, or direct exploration.

items.title: Fast Travel and Difficulty Regions
items.description: Fast travel is available through the world map. Regions have minimum and maximum difficulty ranges that are set when first entered, and the southern areas generally bring harder challenges.

charactersFactions.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Characters and Factions
charactersFactions.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker story centers on Coen, his family, and the vampire power structure ruling Vale Sangora. These character cards summarize the main revealed figures and factions players will encounter.
charactersFactions.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker introduces a 14th-century world where vampires step out of the shadows and become rulers. Coen's struggle to save his family places him between humans, monsters, villagers, possible allies, and the Vrakhiri, a clique of ancient vampires led by Brencis.

items.title: Coen
items.description: The protagonist of The Blood of Dawnwalker. Coen is a young man turned into a Dawnwalker, human by day and vampire by night, fighting to save his family while deciding whether to protect his humanity or embrace cursed power.

items.title: Lunka
items.description: Coen's Black Death-infected sister, introduced in the demo prologue. Brencis' intervention saves her from execution and recovery comes at the cost of vampire rule tightening over Coen's village.

items.title: Brencis
items.description: The leader of the Vrakhiri and the new ruler of Vale Sangora. Born in the Roman Empire as Caeso Burrienus Laurentius, he is hungry for power and central to Coen's family crisis.

items.title: Xanthe
items.description: The oldest vampire in the group and one of the most ancient figures revealed so far. Once connected to Ancient Greece, she carries terrifying knowledge, blood-control power, and a presence that mixes wisdom with dread.

items.title: Ambrus
items.description: The youngest vampire of Brencis' pack. Charming, deceptive, and dangerous, Ambrus presents himself like nobility while carrying resentment from a harsh human past.

items.title: Bakir
items.description: One of Brencis' most powerful lieutenants. A warmonger with a brutal past across the steppes of Central Asia, Bakir is defined by cruelty, bloodlust, and the desire to be feared.

items.title: The Vrakhiri
items.description: The vampire clique that rises to the top of human society in Vale Sangora after the old order collapses. They are the valley's new masters, but player choices may reveal more than a simple enemy faction.

items.title: Blood Guards
items.description: Humans tainted with vampire blood who serve alongside the Vrakhiri. They appear in gameplay as dangerous enemies guarding vampire interests in key locations such as the cathedral sequence.

items.title: Gremla
items.description: A villager shown in the extended demo as someone Coen can choose to help or ignore. Her fate demonstrates how side objectives and delayed action can create visible consequences in The Blood of Dawnwalker.

items.title: Esme
items.description: Coen's mother, referenced during the demo's consequence sequence. Her sickness and possible fate underline how family, time pressure, and player priorities are tied together.

vampiresMonsters.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Vampires and Monsters
vampiresMonsters.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker bestiary includes vampire rulers, failed vampires, undead, mine-dwelling creatures, and cursed places across Vale Sangora.
vampiresMonsters.intro: The Blood of Dawnwalker turns Vale Sangora into a land where vampires have stepped out of the shadows and other creatures of the night follow their lead. Coen faces the Vrakhiri at the top of society, while the wider bestiary includes murohni, kobolds, uriashi, likhos, psoglavs, undead threats, and horrors hidden in ruins, mines, forests, and cursed landmarks.

items.title: Vrakhiri
items.summary: The Vrakhiri are the vampire masters of Vale Sangora.
items.details: Once hidden in the shadows, the Vrakhiri now sit at the top of human society. Their rule reshapes the valley after Brencis rises as the new power at the center of Vale Sangora.

items.title: Brencis
items.summary: Brencis is the leader of the Vrakhiri and the new ruler of Vale Sangora.
items.details: Born in the Roman Empire as Caeso Burrienus Laurentius, Brencis is driven by power, rule, and old ambitions. His control over the valley makes him one of the defining forces behind Coen's journey.

items.title: Xanthe
items.summary: Xanthe is described as the oldest vampire in the Vrakhiri group.
items.details: Her past reaches back to Ancient Greece, where she once served as a priestess. Her age and understanding of the world's deeper nature make her one of the strangest figures among the vampire elite.

items.title: Ambrus
items.summary: Ambrus is the youngest vampire in the pack and has something to prove.
items.details: He sought vampiric power to rise above his human life. Charming, deceptive, and dangerous, he represents the ambitious side of the vampire court.

items.title: Bakir
items.summary: Bakir is one of Brencis' most powerful lieutenants.
items.details: A brutal warmonger with a history of pillage and bloodshed, Bakir is presented as cruel, bloodthirsty, and eager for people to fear his name.

items.title: Blood Hunger
items.summary: Coen's blood hunger can push him toward a loss of control.
items.details: When the blood hunger meter falls too low, Coen is consumed by hunger and dialogue can collapse into a forced outcome. This makes vampirism part of both combat and narrative consequence.

items.title: Murohni
items.summary: Murohni are identified as failed vampires in the announced bestiary.
items.details: They sit between the vampire fantasy and the monster roster, expanding the game beyond noble vampire antagonists into more distorted forms of vampiric horror.

items.title: Kobolds
items.summary: Kobolds are connected with underground mines in Vale Sangora.
items.details: Their presence supports the darker exploration loop, especially in mines, caves, and old underground spaces where the valley's wealth and hidden dangers overlap.

items.title: Uriashi
items.summary: Uriashi are described as mountain folk within the wider creature roster.
items.details: They fit the Carpathian setting of Vale Sangora, where forests, steep mountains, isolated settlements, and old legends shape the threats Coen can encounter.

items.title: Likhos and Psoglavs
items.summary: Likhos and psoglavs are named among the creatures players can find in The Blood of Dawnwalker.
items.details: These monsters point toward a bestiary rooted in gothic horror, Slavic myth, and medieval legend, giving the world more variety than vampires alone.

items.title: Undead in Ancient Ruins
items.summary: Undead creatures are said to hide in ancient ruins.
items.details: The ruins of Vale Sangora are not just scenery. They connect exploration, lost history, and monster encounters as Coen uncovers truths that should remain hidden.

items.title: Cursed Places
items.summary: The Howling Keep, underground corridors below Svartrau, and Shrike's Crag frame the valley as a land of local legends and danger.
items.details: Vale Sangora's myths include hidden mansions, cursed quarries, old cellars, damp caves, timeless ruins, and horrors that locals warn each other to avoid.

trailersVideos.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Trailers and Gameplay Videos
trailersVideos.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker official videos cover the story trailer, Road to Launch stream, gameplay overviews, and reveal footage.
trailersVideos.intro: The official Dawnwalker media lineup covers story, systems, combat, day-and-night gameplay, release information, and developer commentary. Start with the latest story trailer and Road to Launch Event, then use gameplay overview videos to understand combat, exploration, narrative choices, and Coen's human-by-day, vampire-by-night identity.

items.title: Official Dawnwalker YouTube Channel
items.focus: Central hub for official Dawnwalker trailers, gameplay overviews, reveal streams, and short video updates.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker - Story Trailer
items.focus: Introduces allies, enemies, and key story stakes around Coen's journey across Vale Sangora.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker - Road to Launch Event
items.focus: Includes new gameplay footage, story details, system requirements, edition details, developer commentary, and a broader release-date presentation.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker - Gameplay Overview Part III
items.focus: Official gameplay overview focused on open-world activities, quest flow, combat, and the state of the game closer to launch.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker - Gameplay Overview Part II
items.focus: Shows updated gameplay footage around Svartrau, including combat flow, camera improvements, lore-driven exploration, and Coen's dual identity.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker - Gameplay Overview
items.focus: Explains the story-driven open-world RPG structure, day-and-night gameplay, sword fighting, vampiric powers, and player choice.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker - Gameplay Reveal Event
items.focus: Full reveal event centered on combat, spells, vampiric powers, world exploration, and the gameplay difference between day and night.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker - Game Trailer: Trapped Between Two Worlds
items.focus: Presents Coen as a Dawnwalker trapped between humanity and vampiric power, setting up the central fantasy of the game.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker - Cinematic Trailer and Gameplay Teaser
items.focus: Early cinematic reveal and gameplay tease for the dark fantasy vampire RPG world.

multiplayerSinglePlayer.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Multiplayer and Single Player
multiplayerSinglePlayer.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker is positioned as a story-driven single-player dark fantasy action RPG, with PlayStation listing offline play and 1 player support.
multiplayerSinglePlayer.intro: Players searching for multiplayer, co-op, or online features mainly need a clear answer: the official positioning focuses on a solo narrative RPG experience. The game is built around Coen's choices, family rescue, day-and-night abilities, time costs, alliances, and consequences rather than shared online play.

items.question: Is The Blood of Dawnwalker single player?
items.answer: Yes. Official descriptions position The Blood of Dawnwalker as a single-player open-world dark fantasy action RPG with a strong focus on story and narrative.

items.question: Does The Blood of Dawnwalker PS5 support offline play?
items.answer: Yes. The PlayStation Store listing shows offline play enabled and 1 player for the PS5 version.

items.question: Does The Blood of Dawnwalker have co-op or online multiplayer?
items.answer: The official product pages focus on solo play, offline play, and one-player support. They do not present the game as a co-op, PvP, MMO, or online multiplayer RPG.

items.question: What does go alone or forge unlikely alliances mean in The Blood of Dawnwalker?
items.answer: It refers to narrative choices and in-world allies. The phrase describes how Coen can approach quests, relationships, and factions inside the story, not a confirmed online party system.

items.question: Can Xbox players access The Blood of Dawnwalker on console and PC?
items.answer: Yes. The Xbox page lists Xbox Play Anywhere, meaning a single purchase can be played on Xbox console and PC at no additional cost within that program.

items.question: What is The Blood of Dawnwalker main gameplay focus instead of multiplayer?
items.answer: The focus is player choice, consequences, time pressure, day-and-night gameplay, sword combat, magic, vampiric powers, exploration, and how the world reacts to Coen's actions.

rebelWolves.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Rebel Wolves
rebelWolves.subtitle: The Blood of Dawnwalker is developed by Rebel Wolves and published by Bandai Namco Entertainment as the first chapter of a new dark fantasy role-playing saga.
rebelWolves.intro: Rebel Wolves presents The Blood of Dawnwalker as the studio's first game and the opening chapter of a brand-new role-playing saga. The project combines a story-driven single-player RPG focus, Unreal Engine 5 technology, Bandai Namco publishing support, and a team built around experienced developers and passionate newcomers.

items.title: Developer: Rebel Wolves
items.summary: Rebel Wolves is the developer of The Blood of Dawnwalker.
items.details: The studio describes itself as born out of love for RPGs and presents The Blood of Dawnwalker as its first game and the first chapter of a new role-playing saga.

items.title: Publisher: Bandai Namco Entertainment
items.summary: Bandai Namco Entertainment publishes The Blood of Dawnwalker.
items.details: Official game pages list Bandai Namco Entertainment as publisher and Rebel Wolves as developer, with the game coming to PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.

items.title: First Chapter of a New Saga
items.summary: The Blood of Dawnwalker is positioned as the beginning of a brand-new dark fantasy RPG saga.
items.details: Official pages describe The Blood of Dawnwalker as the first chapter of Rebel Wolves' new role-playing saga, built around Coen, Vale Sangora, vampires, choices, and consequences.

items.title: Single-Player Narrative RPG Identity
items.summary: The Blood of Dawnwalker is centered on story, narrative, and player agency.
items.details: The game is described as a single-player open-world dark fantasy action RPG with a strong focus on story and narrative. Its design emphasizes choices, alliances, time pressure, and consequences.

items.title: Unreal Engine 5
items.summary: The Blood of Dawnwalker is built with Unreal Engine 5.
items.details: Official pages connect the game to a next-generation visual presentation powered by Unreal Engine 5 across PC, PlayStation, and Xbox.

items.title: RPG Experience and Team Background
items.summary: The Blood of Dawnwalker comes from a team with deep RPG experience.
items.details: Rebel Wolves highlights teamwork, fairness, transparency, and a shared love of RPGs, with a team that includes developers known for major open-world RPG work.

faq.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker
faq.subtitle: Core questions about The Blood of Dawnwalker release date, platforms, gameplay, story, and editions.

questions.question: When is The Blood of Dawnwalker release date?
questions.answer: The Blood of Dawnwalker launches on September 3, 2026 for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.

questions.question: What kind of game is The Blood of Dawnwalker?
questions.answer: The Blood of Dawnwalker is a single-player dark fantasy open-world action RPG set in 14th-century Europe.

questions.question: Who is Coen in The Blood of Dawnwalker?
questions.answer: Coen is the protagonist of The Blood of Dawnwalker, a Dawnwalker who is human by day and vampire by night.

questions.question: Does The Blood of Dawnwalker have multiplayer?
questions.answer: The Blood of Dawnwalker is positioned as a single-player RPG with official pages focused on solo play and one-player support.

questions.question: What platforms support The Blood of Dawnwalker?
questions.answer: The Blood of Dawnwalker is planned for PC, Steam, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Xbox Play Anywhere support through Xbox.

questions.question: What editions does The Blood of Dawnwalker offer?
questions.answer: The Blood of Dawnwalker has Standard Edition, Eclipse Edition, Collector's Edition, and a retail Day One Edition.
